seo.title: 100 AI Code Generation resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical AI Code Generation resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource provides a technical roadmap for developers and engineering leads to implement AI code generation within professional workflows. It focuses on agentic CLI tools, automated security validation for synthetic code, and context-injection techniques to ensure production-grade output.

sections.heading: Agentic Coding Tools and CLI Assistants

items.title: Aider
items.description: A command-line chat tool that allows you to edit code in your local git repository. It uses a whole-repo map to provide context and automatically commits changes with descriptive messages.

items.title: Claude Code
items.description: Anthropic's agentic CLI tool designed for complex refactoring and multi-file edits. It excels at understanding project-wide dependencies and executing shell commands to validate its own changes.

items.title: Continue.dev
items.description: An open-source IDE extension for VS Code and JetBrains that allows you to swap out LLM backends (Ollama, Anthropic, TogetherAI) and build custom autocomplete providers.

items.title: Cursor
items.description: A fork of VS Code that integrates LLMs at the editor level. It features 'Composer' mode for multi-file generation and native indexing of local documentation for context-aware queries.

items.title: Plandex
items.description: A terminal-based AI coding engine that handles large-scale tasks by breaking them into a plan, executing them in a protected sandbox, and allowing for diff-based reviews.

items.title: OpenDevin / OpenHands
items.description: An open-source platform for software engineering agents that can interact with the browser, terminal, and editor to solve GitHub issues autonomously.

items.title: Mentat
items.description: A CLI tool that coordinates AI-driven code changes by scanning your project and allowing you to select specific files to include in the LLM's context window.

items.title: Cody by Sourcegraph
items.description: An assistant that uses Sourcegraph's code graph to provide context from large, distributed codebases that don't fit into a standard local context window.

items.title: Goose
items.description: An open-source agent from Block (formerly Square) that uses a 'toolkit' approach to allow the AI to interact with Jira, GitHub, and local build tools.

items.title: Gpt-pilot
items.description: An agentic tool focused on the initial scaffolding phase, asking clarifying questions to generate a full-stack application structure from a single prompt.

items.title: Semgrep AI
items.description: Use Semgrep's assistant to scan AI-generated code for common reachability patterns and vulnerabilities that standard static analysis might miss.

items.title: CodiumAI (Cover Agent)
items.description: An automated tool that takes AI-generated code and attempts to reach 100% test coverage by generating Pytest or Jest suites based on the implementation.

items.title: Snyk Code
items.description: Integrate Snyk into the CI/CD pipeline to specifically flag 'hallucinated' dependencies—libraries suggested by AI that do not actually exist in public registries.

items.title: Promptfoo
items.description: A CLI tool to run test cases against your code generation prompts. Use it to ensure that model updates don't break specific coding style or logic requirements.

items.title: DeepSource Autofix
items.description: An automated code review tool that identifies bug risks and security flaws, providing an AI-generated suggestion to fix the issue directly in the PR.

items.title: Giskard
items.description: An open-source framework for testing LLM outputs, useful for validating that AI-generated SQL or API integration code meets specific business logic constraints.

items.title: Bandit
items.description: A security linter for Python that should be run as a pre-commit hook to catch common AI mistakes like hardcoded passwords or insecure 'eval()' calls.

items.title: TruLens-Eval
items.description: A library to evaluate RAG-based coding assistants by measuring 'groundedness'—ensuring the AI only uses the provided documentation and not outdated training data.

items.title: Bouncer
items.description: A validation tool that uses JSON Schema or Pydantic models to force LLMs to return structured code metadata instead of conversational text.

items.title: SonarQube AI Code Assurance
items.description: A set of quality gates designed to track the percentage of AI-generated code in a repository and enforce stricter linting rules on those blocks.

items.title: .cursorrules Configuration
items.description: Create a .cursorrules file in your project root to define specific architectural patterns, naming conventions, and libraries the AI must use.

items.title: Repomix
items.description: A tool that bundles your entire codebase into a single, LLM-friendly text file, optimized with headers to help models understand the directory structure.

items.title: Tree-sitter Integration
items.description: Use Tree-sitter to parse code into Abstract Syntax Trees (AST) before sending it to an LLM, allowing for more precise 'code chunking' in RAG systems.

items.title: LlamaIndex Code Splitter
items.description: A specialized text splitter that understands language-specific syntax (Python, JS, Go) to avoid breaking functions or classes across context windows.

items.title: Voy
items.description: A lightweight WASM vector search engine that can be embedded in IDE extensions to provide local, low-latency semantic search of the codebase.

items.title: Ell
items.description: A library that treats prompts as functional code, allowing you to version, test, and trace code generation prompts alongside your application logic.

items.title: Griptape
items.description: A Python framework for building AI workflows that keep code generation tasks 'off-prompt' by using pre-defined tools for file I/O and shell execution.

items.title: Markdown-based System Prompts
items.description: Structure your system prompts using Markdown headers (# Instruction, ## Constraints) to improve model following for complex boilerplate generation.

items.title: Context-Free Grammar (CFG) Sampling
items.description: Use libraries like Outlines to constrain LLM output to valid code syntax, preventing the model from generating syntax errors during high-temperature runs.

items.title: Vector Admin
items.description: A GUI for managing the vector embeddings of your private documentation, ensuring your coding assistant has access to the latest internal API specs.

pro_tips: Always use 'Chain of Thought' prompting for complex logic by adding 'Think step-by-step and write pseudocode first' to your code generation requests.
Implement a strict 'No AI-generated Migrations' policy without manual review to prevent destructive database schema changes.
Use a .clauderules or .cursorrules file to explicitly blacklist deprecated libraries and enforce the use of specific internal wrappers.
Verify every AI-suggested package on npm or PyPI before installing to mitigate the risk of dependency confusion or typosquatting attacks.
For large refactors, use 'Diff-only' mode in CLI agents to reduce token costs and focus the model on the specific lines of code changing.
